text: The surgeon mobilizes the greater omentum from the transverse colon, separating these structures to access the perforated duodenal ulcer site.
Three number one Vicryl sutures on a CT-II needle are introduced and positioned to secure the omental patch over the perforated duodenal ulcer.
Additional sutures are placed to secure the omental patch over the perforated duodenal ulcer, ensuring stability and coverage.

The assistant holds the mobilized patch of omentum in place while the surgeon ties the number one vicryl sutures tightly over the perforated duodenal ulcer, securing the omental patch to the ulcer site.
The surgeon deliberately avoids approximating the edges of the perforated duodenal ulcer, focusing instead on securing the omental patch over the defect.
The surgeon considers placing additional sutures to enhance the seal of the omental patch over the perforated ulcer, ensuring a secure closure.
